calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Continued To Jul-24-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling AS TO DEFENDANTS WARNICK PROPERTIES GROUP, INC. A CALIFORNIA, DAVID BARRETT, ANTHONY KWIECIEN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled. Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations under CCP 338 is overruled. Plaintiffs have pled sufficient facts to show the delayed discovery rule applies as Plaintiffs could not have discovered that Defendants had made misrepresentations in the Disclosure Statement until they discovered the water damage in November 2010. (See FAC 45-49.) Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations under Cal. Civ. Code 2079.4 is overruled as Plaintiffs are not challenging the manner with which Defendants inspected the building. Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action fails to meet the heightened pleadings standard for a cause of action of fraud is overruled. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for uncertainty is overruled because Defendants can reasonably determine from the FAC what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts or claims are directed against him or her. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: L. Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling ON DEFENDANTS JOEL GOODRICH, VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. DBA COLDWELL BANKER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled. Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations under CCP 338 is overruled. Plaintiffs have pled sufficient facts to show the delayed discovery rule applies as Plaintiffs could not have discovered that Defendants had made misrepresentations in the Disclosure Statement until they discovered the water damage in November 2010. (See FAC 45-49.) Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations under Cal. Civ. Code 2079.4 is overruled as Plaintiffs are not challenging the manner with which Defendants inspected the building. Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action fails to meet the heightened pleadings standard for a cause of action of fraud is overruled. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for uncertainty is overruled because Defendants can reasonably determine from the FAC what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts or claims are directed against him or her. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for failure to allege sufficient facts to state a claim is overruled. Plaintiffs have alleged that Defendants knew or should have know about the damage to the building because they ordered inspection and has seen or had access to reports from previous inspections that the building had water damage. (See FAC 23.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: L. Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
